title: Patari (service)

([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu "Urdu"): پٹاری) is a [Pakistani](/wiki/Pakistani "Pakistani") [music streaming](/wiki/Comparison_of_on-demand_streaming_music_services "Comparison of on-demand streaming music services") service founded in February 2015 by Khalid Bajwa, [Faisal Sherjan](/w/index.php?title=Faisal_Sherjan&action=edit&redlink=1 "Faisal Sherjan (page does not exist)"), Iqbal Talaat Bhatti and Humayun Haroon.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) The site provides [Pakistani music](/wiki/Pakistani_music "Pakistani music") and is known as the largest local music streaming service in [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

["The visionary ways of Patari | TNS - The News on Sunday"](https://web.archive.org/web/20171125231055/http://tns.thenews.com.pk/visionary-ways-patari/#.Whnjl1WWbIU). *tns.thenews.com.pk*. Archived from [the original](http://tns.thenews.com.pk/visionary-ways-patari/#.Whnjl1WWbIU) on 2017-11-25. Retrieved 2017-11-25.

Here's how to lay your hands on it"](https://web.archive.org/web/20150419093615/http://www.hindustantimes.com/music/love-pakistani-music-here-s-how-to-lay-your-hands-on-it/article1-1338836.aspx). *Hindustan Times*. New Delhi, India. Archived from [the original](http://www.hindustantimes.com/music/love-pakistani-music-here-s-how-to-lay-your-hands-on-it/article1-1338836.aspx) on April 19, 2015. Retrieved 30 June 2015.

Pakistan. Archived from [the original](http://plan9.pitb.gov.pk/patari-contains-the-biggest-pakistani-music-collection-ever/) on 14 May 2015. Retrieved 30 June 2015.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani music streaming website

Patari  
پٹاری

|  |  |

| Headquarters | [Lahore](/wiki/Lahore "Lahore"), Punjab , [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") |

| CEO | Rabeel Warraich (Interim CEO) |
| Key people | * Khalid Bajwa (co-founder and ex-ceo) * Humayun Haroon (co-founder, mobile developer) * Iqbal Talaat Bhatti (co-founder, [CTO](/wiki/Chief_technology_officer "Chief technology officer")) * [Faisal Sherjan](/w/index.php?title=Faisal_Sherjan&action=edit&redlink=1 "Faisal Sherjan (page does not exist)") (co-founder, Chief Strategy Officer) |

| [Native clients on](/wiki/Computing_platform "Computing platform") | * [Windows](/wiki/Microsoft_Windows "Microsoft Windows") * [iOS](/wiki/IOS "IOS") * [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)") |

**Patari** ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu "Urdu"): پٹاری) is a [Pakistani](/wiki/Pakistani "Pakistani") [music streaming](/wiki/Comparison_of_on-demand_streaming_music_services "Comparison of on-demand streaming music services") service founded in February 2015 by Khalid Bajwa, [Faisal Sherjan](/w/index.php?title=Faisal_Sherjan&action=edit&redlink=1 "Faisal Sherjan (page does not exist)"), Iqbal Talaat Bhatti and Humayun Haroon.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) The site provides [Pakistani music](/wiki/Pakistani_music "Pakistani music") and is known as the largest local music streaming service in [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Patari_(service)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Patari_(service)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Background")]

According to the chief creative officer of Patari, the initial plan was to create an online [Pakistani drama serial](/wiki/Pakistani_dramas "Pakistani dramas") portal. However, when this did not succeed, the [Jang Group](/wiki/Jang_Group "Jang Group")’s Chief Strategy Officer Faisal Sherjan, encouraged Patari's founders to focus on music instead.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### Launch

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Patari_(service)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Launch")]

Patari was launched in February 2015 in beta version with a collection of 20,000 songs and 600 artists and was accessible only through invites and later by registration, which led to an increase in access across [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").[[10]](#cite_note-10) “We honestly just kept our heads down and kept working on it. Just another day in the life of a start-up we thought, and the next thing we knew, it was everywhere. People were sharing screenshots, making memes, trading tips on how to get invites and reaching out to us to tell us how much they love Pakistani music and Patari” said Khalid Bajwa.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) Patari was opened to public browsing on 4 September 2015.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

### Apps

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Patari_(service)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Apps")]

Patari launched [apps](/wiki/Mobile_app "Mobile app") for [iOS](/wiki/IOS "IOS") and [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)") in August 2015. It is planning to launch a Windows OS [Windows Mobile](/wiki/Windows_Mobile "Windows Mobile") app in the near future.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

### Funding

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Patari_(service)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Funding")]

On 30 December 2016, Patari received a total amount of US$200,000 in seed funding from the investment firm *Sarmayacar*.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### Weekly singles chart

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Patari_(service)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Weekly singles chart")]

*Patari Haftanama* is a [Pakistani](/wiki/Pakistani "Pakistani") top 20 [singles chart](/wiki/Singles_chart "Singles chart") that is presented every Thursday at [Patari.pk](/wiki/Patari.pk "Patari.pk").[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Legal issues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Patari_(service)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Legal issues")]

In June 2015, [EMI](/wiki/EMI "EMI") Pakistan requested Patari to remove all its content from the website as it held the licenses for over 60,000 Pakistani artists and controlled almost 70% of the total music of Pakistan. Zeeshan Chaudhry, General Manager of EMI Pakistan said: “I am not against Patari. I am against any portal or platform that provides illegal music.” Patari and EMI have since resolved their differences and signed an agreement that places EMI on the Patari platform.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)

In April 2019, Patari co-founder and CEO Khalid Bajwa was accused of sexual harassment by several woman working at Patari leading to his resignation.[[24]](#cite_note-24)
